channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.
The security f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-linked JDY botnet expands targeting of U.S. military networks
item.description: The JDY botnet, a malware network previously associated with Chinese threat actors like Volt Typhoon, has significantly expanded its targeting scope and reconnaissance efforts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitati...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The 5 Best Practices for Secure Identity Verification
item.description: Attackers are increasingly bypassing weak authentication through phishing, MFA fatigue, and service desk social engineering. Specops Software breaks down five best practices for stronger identity veri...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strateg...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Acc...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft: Some Windows PCs fail to install latest monthly updates
item.description: Microsoft warned customers on Tuesday that they may have issues installing the latest monthly updates on some Windows devices that were upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description:
Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem.
Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report loo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft patches YellowKey, GreenPlasma, MiniPlasma zero-days
item.description: On Tuesday, Microsoft patched two zero-day vulnerabilities that let attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems, and a third one that grants access to BitLocker-protected drives....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release.
Of the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards
item.description: On June 9, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, the most capable model it has ever made, generally available. It also did something unusual: it shipped one model as two products, split not by capability...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
item.description:
ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances.
"On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied a s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ivanti: Max severity Sentry flaw allows code execution as root
item.description: Ivanti has patched two critical vulnerabilities in its Sentry secure mobile gateway solution, including a maximum-severity flaw that enables remote attackers to execute code with root privileges. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
item.description: The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named RoguePlanet....
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited, could re...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic rolls out Claude Fable 5, but it's available for a limited time
item.description: Anthropic has begun rolling out a new model called "Fable," which is based on the same underlying model as Mythos, its most powerful AI model class. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Defender 'RoguePlanet' zero-day grants SYSTEM privileges
item.description: A security researcher has released a new Microsoft Defender zero-day exploit named "RoguePlanet" just hours after Microsoft fixed two previously disclosed flaws during June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesda...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyber War Is Reshaping Everyday Life
item.description: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyber attacks threaten hospitals, utilities and essential services....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
item.description: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data
item.description: ServiceNow is warning about a security incident after attackers exploited an unauthenticated access flaw through a vulnerable API endpoint, allowing them to query data from customer instances. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenClaw AI agent found falling for phishing attacks, spills user data
item.description: Phishing simulation on an OpenClaw email agent with various configuration profiles showed that it was susceptible to tactics commonly used to compromise human users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
item.description: “Ghost-Sender" is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: SAP fixes critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
item.description: SAP has released fixes for 15 vulnerabilities as part of its June 2026 Security Patch package, including four critical-severity flaws affecting SAP NetWeaver and SAP Commerce Cloud. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
item.description: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasmi attack on Microsoft last month....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5094127 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5094127 extended security update, which fixes the June 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and adds new functionality to monitor the rollout of updated Secure Bo...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 6 zero-days, 200 flaws
item.description: Today is Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday, with security updates for 200 flaws, including five publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities and one actively exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 KB5094126 & KB5093998 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5094126 and KB5093998 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
